description_long: This object consists of two disposable cups of different sizes, both with a light beige exterior and adorned with green leaf motifs and subtle branding. The larger cup has a fitted white lid, while the smaller cup is open at the top. Both cups feature a smooth, slightly glossy surface and are made from thin, lightweight paper or cardboard, typical of takeaway food containers. The branding includes the word 'CENTRALE' and a small logo, with additional text and symbols visible on the front. The cups are cylindrical with gently flared rims, and the decorative leaf pattern wraps around the lower portion of each cup. These cups are commonly used for serving ice cream, soup, or other takeaway foods in urban environments.
description: A pair of disposable cups with white lids and green leaf motifs, featuring branding and a smooth, slightly glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both cups side by side, with branding, logo, and green leaf motifs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view of the larger cup, highlighting the leaf motif and the fitted white lid, with minimal branding visible.
description_view_2: Back view of both cups, showing the plain beige surface with faint leaf patterns and less visible branding.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the open tops of both cups, emphasizing their cylindrical shape and the white interior.
